questions.prompt: What does it mean to 'arm' a drone?
options: Making the motors active (ready to fly); when you apply throttle, the motors spin
Charging the drone's battery
Removing the propellers
Turning off the drone and putting it back in its box
questions.explanation: Arming activates the motors: once armed, the motors spin when you apply throttle. Disarming locks the motors (they won't spin even if you apply throttle).

questions.prompt: Where is the arming switch usually assigned on the radio, and why is it important?
options: To the throttle stick; it protects the battery
To a switch on an AUX (auxiliary) channel; it gives you the power to disarm instantly
To the screen brightness button; it sharpens the image
It isn't assigned anywhere; arming is automatic
questions.explanation: Arming is bound to a switch on an AUX channel. If something goes wrong, you can flip that switch off and stop the motors instantly; this is the most important safety reflex.

questions.prompt: What is the most important safety rule when testing a drone on the bench or arming it for the first time?
options: Keeping the battery fully charged
Removing the propellers first (prop-off testing)
Turning off the room lights
Disconnecting the radio from the computer
questions.explanation: Spinning propellers are like sharp blades. Removing the propellers before testing/setup (prop-off) lets you safely see the motors spin without hurting anyone.

questions.prompt: On most drones, when do the motors start spinning?
options: Only when the throttle is fully open
As soon as the battery is plugged in, before arming
At the moment of arming, even at zero throttle, at a very low idle speed
Never; the propellers are turned by hand
questions.explanation: The motors 'wake up' at the moment of arming, not with throttle. It's normal for them to start spinning at a low idle even at zero throttle once armed, showing the drone is awaiting commands.

questions.prompt: If the link between the radio and the drone is lost while flying, what is the safest failsafe behavior for a beginner?
options: The drone continuing to fly forward at full throttle
The motors stopping, so that the drone falls where it is instead of flying away uncontrollably
The drone automatically ejecting its propellers
Nothing; the drone keeps flying the same way
questions.explanation: Failsafe kicks in when the link is lost. The safest behavior is for the motors to cut, so an out-of-control drone drops instead of flying off into the distance.
